scope: Planning only. This plan does not apply, rewrite, repair, rename, delete, or mark any database migration; it does not create business data.
governingRules: Do not alter, delete, rename, re-date, squash, or retroactively push any historical migration file.
Do not write directly to Supabase's migration ledger or mark a missing migration as applied.
Allocate one new migration version greater than every repository and staging version at the moment it is authored.
Keep the reconciliation migration additive and idempotent, and execute its DDL in one transaction where PostgreSQL permits it.
Do not replay the 63 historical gaps as a batch. Reconcile only the MVP atomic-creation contract proven necessary for pilot operation.
If a preflight finds a conflicting object shape or legacy duplicate data, abort before DDL and prepare a later correction migration; do not weaken or rewrite history.

reconciliationTarget.purpose: Expose one durable and idempotent lead/accepted-offer to transaction creation boundary for the controlled MVP pilot.
minimumSchema: ten missing transaction fields identified in Phase 1B
participant, document, and workflow bootstrap helpers
explicit privilege hardening for every security-definer helper and the atomic RPC
excluded: Replaying unrelated document-generator, calendar, notification, or attorney migrations from the missing ranges
Backfilling historical transactions unless a separate approved migration requires it
Changing production exposure, notification modes, or user data

requiredPreflightBeforeAuthoring: Re-read the linked staging ledger and assert the selected new version is monotonic.

Inspect actual staging constraints/indexes for transaction_participants(transaction_id, role_type, legal_role), transaction_required_documents(transaction_id, document_key), and transaction_workflow_lanes(transaction_id, lane_type). Do not assume the historical conflict targets are valid.
Check for duplicate existing rows that would prevent any required unique index or constraint from being added.
Assert expected column data types and nullable/default behaviour for every existing object consumed by the helper functions.
Reconfirm the named RPC remains absent and that no conflicting function overload exists.
Capture a schema/ledger backup and record the before-state in an audit artifact.
authoringSequence: Create a new timestamped reconciliation migration after the current highest version; leave 202607180046 untouched.
Start a transaction and run explicit preflight assertions with clear failure messages.
Add the missing mandate_packet_id prerequisite and atomic transaction fields using additive, idempotent DDL.
Create only indexes/constraints whose exact compatibility and duplicate checks passed; otherwise abort and split the corrective work into a later migration.
Create or replace only the three atomic bootstrap helpers and the single p_payload jsonb RPC after their schema prerequisites exist.
Revoke public execution for every security-definer helper and the target RPC; grant authenticated execution only for the target RPC.
Commit. Any later correction must be a new migration, never an edit of this one.
postApplyAcceptance: The linked ledger contains the new reconciliation version while every historical version remains unchanged.
The named RPC resolves; an invalid empty payload must return a validation/access error, never PGRST202.
Run the authenticated staging atomic-creation smoke transaction only after the RPC contract check passes.
Verify idempotency, lead and offer linkage, participants, documents, lanes, and no partial transaction after a forced validation failure.
Keep pilot exposure paused until those checks have passed and been recorded.
recoveryRule: If application or verification fails, stop exposure and add a new forward correction migration after diagnosis. Do not delete the reconciliation migration, roll back the migration ledger, or rewrite applied SQL history.
nextPhase: Phase 2B — perform the prerequisite constraint/index and duplicate-data preflight against staging, then decide whether one reconciliation migration can safely be authored.
